steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the best description of the overall structure of the text. Look for how each paragraph functions and what the passage moves through from start to finish.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'general interest, specific study, finding': The passage opens with broad scientific interest in shipwrecks' ecological effects, narrows to Rachel Mugge's study using pine and oak samples, then ends with the finding that microbial diversity peaks at 125 meters. That three-part progression is exactly right. ✓
  • Choice B — mentions 'historical significance': The passage says nothing about historical significance of shipwrecks. ✗
  • Choice C — mentions 'wood type influence': Wood type (pine vs. oak) is part of the methodology, not the focus or conclusion of the text. ✗
  • Choice D — mentions 'scholarly attention': The passage does not discuss the level of scholarly attention the topic has received. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
